meta.focus_summary_text:
🔥 Weekly AP Quant Radar — Technical Focus List

[AP-Clock]:
• $POWL 180/180 (rem 0d)
• $PPC 159/180 (rem 21d)

[ 📉 BREAKDOWNS (Below 200-SMA) ]
• $DY | NEWS+DVG+BD200 | -3.0% | Vol 1.3x | Dycom reported strong fiscal Q4 2026 results with revenue of $1.458 billion and backlog of $9.542 billion, both highlighting strong underlying demand. ([Dycom Industries, Inc][2])
• $CRDO | NEWS+DVG+BD200 | -11.9% | Vol 0.7x | Credo beat fiscal Q3 2026 expectations with $407.0 million revenue and $1.07 adjusted EPS, but the stock sold off after results as margin concerns surfaced. ([Investors][3])
• $OKTA | NEWS+BD200 | -12.3% | Vol 0.8x | Okta beat Q4 expectations, but forecast its slowest revenue growth since IPO; BMO later upgraded the stock, citing better revenue durability. ([reuters.com][4])
• $CCL | NEWS+BD200 | -8.5% | Vol 1.3x | ER in 9d
• $CVSA | NEWS+BD200 | -13.0% | Vol 1.1x | Reuters stock-story data continued to show CVSA with a Buy recommendation from covering analysts. ([Reuters][6])
• $SKYW | NEWS+BD200 | -11.6% | Vol 1.2x | Recent coverage continued to show SKYW with a Moderate Buy consensus and analyst targets materially above the current price. ([marketbeat.com][7])
• $EAT | NEWS+DVG+BD200 | -6.9% | Vol 1.0x | Wolfe Research initiated Brinker with an Outperform rating after the company’s strong fiscal Q2 execution. ([Investing.com Canada][8])

[ 🚀 STRENGTH (Above 200-SMA) ]
• $AGX | NEWS+X200 | +57.7% | Vol 1.4x | Argan hit a new high in early March while analyst targets remained below the current price and consensus stayed at Hold. ([marketbeat.com][1])

🎯 Triggers: OKTA > 78.89 (EMA21) | CVSA > 101.06 (EMA21) | CRDO > 117.01 (EMA21)

focus_view_model.news_headline: Dycom reported strong fiscal Q4 2026 results with revenue of $1.458 billion and backlog of $9.542 billion, both highlighting strong underlying demand. ([Dycom Industries, Inc][2])
focus_view_model.news_summary: The external event was clearly positive, but the stock still sits below all key moving averages in the TECH PACK, which turns the failed follow-through itself into the main audit signal. ([Dycom Industries, Inc][2])

logic_pillars.technical: F2/F4/F5 are all bearish: LatestPrice 360.29 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) is below SMA200 374.57, EMA55 383.96 and EMA21 395.69.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: V1 shows RVOL20=1.35, so the breakdown is not quiet drift. Volume is high enough to treat the move as institutionally relevant.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: See external_intel: this was a positive earnings/backlog event, so the inability to sustain strength is more important than the headline beat itself.

focus_view_model.divergence: This is the sharpest sentiment collision in the pack: F6=Buy and external_intel is POSITIVE, yet F3 remains DOWNTREND and price is below F2/F4/F5. That usually signals crowded expectations, sell-the-news behavior, or sector rotation overpowering fundamentals.
focus_view_model.action_plan: Treat LatestPrice 360.29 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) as damaged until price can first reclaim SMA200 374.57. A more convincing repair would require recovering EMA55 383.96 and then EMA21 395.69.
focus_view_model.news_blurb: Dycom reported strong fiscal Q4 2026 results with revenue of $1.458 billion and backlog of $9.542 billion, both highlighting strong underlying demand. ([Dycom Industries, Inc][2]) · 5d · 2 src

focus_view_model.news_headline: Credo beat fiscal Q3 2026 expectations with $407.0 million revenue and $1.07 adjusted EPS, but the stock sold off after results as margin concerns surfaced. ([Investors][3])
focus_view_model.news_summary: This is a classic sell-the-news setup: the reported numbers were strong, but investor reaction turned negative because the market focused on gross-margin pressure and quality of follow-through rather than headline growth alone. ([Investors][3])

logic_pillars.technical: F2/F4/F5 remain bearish-to-fragile: LatestPrice 115.98 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) is below SMA200 127.28, below EMA55 128.17 and slightly below EMA21 117.48.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: V1 shows RVOL20=0.92, which means there is no strong accumulation signal behind the attempted stabilization.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: See external_intel: strong reported growth did not convert into bullish price action, which is why the reaction matters more than the beat.

focus_view_model.divergence: F6=Strong Buy conflicts with both the post-earnings reaction and the position below F2/F4/F5. That gap suggests the market is prioritizing margin quality and expectation reset over raw revenue growth.
focus_view_model.action_plan: Avoid treating LatestPrice 115.98 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) as a clean long trigger unless price can reclaim EMA21 117.48 first and then EMA55 128.17. A full trend repair would still require SMA200 127.28 to be recovered and held.
focus_view_model.news_blurb: Credo beat fiscal Q3 2026 expectations with $407.0 million revenue and $1.07 adjusted EPS, but the stock sold off after results as margin concerns surfaced. ([Investors][3]) · 6d · 2 src

focus_view_model.news_headline: Okta beat Q4 expectations, but forecast its slowest revenue growth since IPO; BMO later upgraded the stock, citing better revenue durability. ([reuters.com][4])
focus_view_model.news_summary: The dominant external signal is still weak near-term guidance, even though at least one analyst upgrade argues that the market may be over-penalizing the slowdown. ([Reuters][4])

logic_pillars.technical: F2/F4 remain bearish while F5 is only marginally constructive: LatestPrice 79.71 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) is below SMA200 90.95, below EMA55 83.00, and only slightly above EMA21 78.82.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: V1 shows RVOL20=0.71, so the bounce lacks institutional confirmation.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: See external_intel: there is a real sentiment collision between weak growth guidance and a fresh analyst upgrade.

focus_view_model.divergence: F3 remains DOWNTREND even after a beat and an upgrade-style headline, which implies the market is putting more weight on slower forward growth than on backward-looking execution.
focus_view_model.action_plan: Watch whether LatestPrice 79.71 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) can hold above EMA21 78.82 and then reclaim EMA55 83.00. Without an EMA55 83.00 break, the broader downtrend remains intact.
focus_view_model.news_blurb: Okta beat Q4 expectations, but forecast its slowest revenue growth since IPO; BMO later upgraded the stock, citing better revenue durability. ([reuters.com][4]) · 4d · 2 src

focus_view_model.news_headline: Argan hit a new high in early March while analyst targets remained below the current price and consensus stayed at Hold. ([marketbeat.com][1])
focus_view_model.news_summary: The stock has powerful momentum, but the external setup is now dominated by extension risk because price is well above the average analyst target and recent coverage still points to mixed ratings rather than broad fundamental re-rating. ([marketbeat.com][1])

logic_pillars.technical: F2/F4/F5 confirm a very extended uptrend: LatestPrice 466.38 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) is above SMA200 289.57, EMA55 386.42 and EMA21 424.46.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: V1 shows RVOL20=1.81, which confirms real participation; the move is strong, but at this extension level high volume can also reflect late-stage chasing rather than fresh low-risk entry.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: See external_intel: the key issue is not a missing catalyst but a valuation/target gap emerging against an already parabolic tape.

focus_view_model.divergence: F3 shows a clean UPTREND while external_intel is only NEUTRAL. F6=Hold is weaker than price action, which suggests the tape is outrunning analyst comfort rather than receiving broad rating confirmation.
focus_view_model.action_plan: Do not chase strength blindly; monitor whether LatestPrice 466.38 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) can continue to hold above EMA21 424.46. A loss of EMA21 424.46 would be the first clear fatigue signal, while EMA55 386.42 is the deeper trend support.
focus_view_model.news_blurb: Argan hit a new high in early March while analyst targets remained below the current price and consensus stayed at Hold. ([marketbeat.com][1]) · 5d · 2 src

focus_view_model.news_headline: Cruise stocks were pressured by higher fuel-cost fears as oil surged on Middle East tensions, with the sector repricing macro input risk. ([Reuters][5])
focus_view_model.news_summary: The relevant external event is not company-specific execution but a sector-level fuel-cost shock; in a RISK_OFF regime, that macro pressure can dominate otherwise constructive cruise demand narratives. ([Reuters][5])

logic_pillars.technical: F2/F4/F5 are all bearish: LatestPrice 26.39 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) is below SMA200 28.61, EMA55 29.95 and EMA21 29.74.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: V1 shows RVOL20=2.09, which is one of the strongest conviction signals in the pack and validates the downside move.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: See external_intel: macro energy pressure is the dominant catalyst, not a stock-specific earnings surprise.

focus_view_model.divergence: Even if analysts remain constructive on the longer-term cruise story, the market is currently trading CCL as a fuel-cost sensitivity vehicle. In the present regime, macro cost shocks are overpowering bullish longer-term narratives.
focus_view_model.action_plan: No constructive action until LatestPrice 26.39 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) can reclaim EMA21 29.74; a stronger repair would also require EMA55 29.95 to be recovered on better volume confirmation.
focus_view_model.news_blurb: Cruise stocks were pressured by higher fuel-cost fears as oil surged on Middle East tensions, with the sector repricing macro input risk. ([Reuters][5]) · today · 3 src

focus_view_model.news_headline: Reuters stock-story data continued to show CVSA with a Buy recommendation from covering analysts. ([Reuters][6])
focus_view_model.news_summary: There is no strong fresh event headline in the last week, but analyst framing remains constructive, which matters because the chart is trying to stabilize just above EMA21 while still below EMA55 and SMA200. ([Reuters][6])

logic_pillars.technical: F5 is modestly constructive, but F2/F4 still dominate the bigger picture: LatestPrice 102.05 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-06) is above EMA21 100.86, below EMA55 104.22 and below SMA200 118.72.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: V1 shows RVOL20=0.94, so this looks more like quiet stabilization than decisive institutional accumulation.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: See external_intel: the support is mainly rating-based rather than event-driven.

focus_view_model.divergence: F6=Buy aligns with external analyst tone, but F3 still says DOWNTREND and price has not yet reclaimed F4. That makes this a watchlist stabilization setup, not a confirmed trend reversal.
focus_view_model.action_plan: Constructive only if LatestPrice 102.05 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-06) can reclaim and hold above EMA55 104.22; until then, SMA200 118.72 remains the larger overhead trend barrier.
focus_view_model.news_blurb: Reuters stock-story data continued to show CVSA with a Buy recommendation from covering analysts. ([Reuters][6]) · 4d · 1 src

focus_view_model.news_headline: Recent coverage continued to show SKYW with a Moderate Buy consensus and analyst targets materially above the current price. ([marketbeat.com][7])
focus_view_model.news_summary: The external backdrop is still fundamentally supportive at the analyst level, but the stock has not translated that support into trend repair and remains vulnerable to broader travel/airline macro pressure. ([marketbeat.com][7])

logic_pillars.technical: F2/F4/F5 are all bearish: LatestPrice 93.20 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) is below SMA200 104.12, EMA55 101.56 and EMA21 100.87.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: V1 shows RVOL20=1.74, which signals that the recent weakness is active and should not be dismissed as low-volume drift.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: See external_intel: analyst support exists, but it is not yet producing price confirmation.

focus_view_model.divergence: This is another clean divergence case: positive external framing and above-market targets are not enough while F3 stays DOWNTREND and V1 is elevated. Macro travel sensitivity appears to be dominating stock-specific optimism.
focus_view_model.action_plan: Wait for LatestPrice 93.20 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) to reclaim EMA21 100.87 before assuming analyst optimism is starting to matter in the tape.
focus_view_model.news_blurb: Recent coverage continued to show SKYW with a Moderate Buy consensus and analyst targets materially above the current price. ([marketbeat.com][7]) · 7d · 2 src

focus_view_model.news_headline: Wolfe Research initiated Brinker with an Outperform rating after the company’s strong fiscal Q2 execution. ([Investing.com Canada][8])
focus_view_model.news_summary: The freshest relevant event is supportive new analyst coverage, but the chart remains weak enough that this looks more like a valuation/expectation reset than an active momentum restart. ([Investing.com Canada][8])

logic_pillars.technical: F2/F4/F5 remain bearish: LatestPrice 137.57 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) is below SMA200 150.15, EMA55 149.97 and EMA21 147.40.
logic_pillars.volume_analysis: V1 shows RVOL20=0.80, which means there is not yet enough participation to validate a durable reversal attempt.
logic_pillars.news_catalyst: See external_intel: the latest event is analyst support, but price still has not confirmed it.

focus_view_model.divergence: F6=Buy and fresh positive coverage lean constructive, but F3 plus the position below F2/F4/F5 say the tape is still digesting prior gains and resetting expectations.
focus_view_model.action_plan: Do not treat LatestPrice 137.57 (type=Close, asof=2026-03-09) as repaired unless price can first reclaim EMA21 147.40 and then EMA55 149.97.
focus_view_model.news_blurb: Wolfe Research initiated Brinker with an Outperform rating after the company’s strong fiscal Q2 execution. ([Investing.com Canada][8]) · today · 2 src
